title: scleranthus × intermedius nothosubsp. fasciculatus
intro: Scleranthus × intermedius nothosubsp. fasciculatus is a nothosubspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the knawel or prickly scorpion grass. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Scleranthus × intermedius nothosubsp. fasciculatus include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and aiding in the treatment of various skin conditions. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use through the infusion of its leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as a tea or used in herbal preparations, such as salves and creams.
